page.title: Coeliac Disease (Refractory / RCD) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Celiac Disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Celiac Disease: 103 alterations and 73 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Breastfeeding seems to offer a protection against the development of CD in predisposed infants.
Cesarean delivery is associated with celiac disease in children.
Rates of cesarian section and miscarriage are higher in those with celiac disease.
Recurrent miscarriages may occur due to undiagnosed celiac disease, and may resolve after a gluten-free diet.
Consumption of oats does not induce TG2 autoantibody production at mucosal level in children with celiac disease.
"Frequency of celiac disease and irritable bowel syndrome coexistance and its influence on the disease course"
2-5% of celiac disease does not respond to a gluten-free diet and may lead to the development of enteropathy-associated T-cell lymphoma.
6 months of a gluten free diet result in improvements in celiac disease patients with hypertransaminasemia liver involvement
A gluten free diet could be a useful approach in reducing GERD symptoms in adult celiac patients.
A glutenfree diet showed short-term benefits by reducing GI symptoms and severe hypoglycaemia in T1D and CD
A high frequency of celiac disease was found in patients with lymphoma in the southeast region of Turkey.
A significant percentage of the newly diagnosed patients with CD have macroamylasemia.
A significant proportion of patients with migraine may have celiac disease, and that a gluten free diet may lead to a improvement in the migraine in these patients.
A subgroup of Irritable Bowel Syndrome patients have latent/potential celiac disease and respond to a gluten-free diet.
ADHD sympomatology is markedly over represented among untreated CD patients and that a gluten-free diet may improve symptoms significantly within a short period of time.
Adults with Type 1 diabetes have a higher prevalance of celiac disease associated antibodies.
B vitamin supplementation reduces plasma homocysteine levels in Celiac disease.
B-vitamin status and homocysteine levels are important to monitor in celiac disease.
Bromelain-loaded nanocomposites decrease inflammatory and cytotoxicity effects of gliadin on Caco-2 cells and peripheral blood mononuclear cells of celiac patients.
Cardiomyopathy in patients with celiac disease may be completely reversible with a gluten-free diet.
Carnitine and selenium levels are decreased in children with celiac disease.
Celiac disease has a far higher prevalence in autoimmune thyroid disorders.
Celiac disease has a high prevalance rate in autoimmune cholestatic liver disorders.
Celiac disease has been associated with autoimmune myocarditis.
Celiac disease has been associated with recurrent guillain barre syndrome.
Celiac disease is a common condition in Down syndrome.
Celiac disease is associated with ataxic syndromes without definite diagnosis, suggesting that it plays a part in the pathogenesis of some ataxic syndromes.
Celiac disease is associated with restless legs syndrome.
Celiac disease is far higher in those diagnosed with Grave's disease.
Celiac disease is found in a far higher percentage of patients with end-stage autoimmune liver disease.
Celiac disease is markedly overrepresented among patients presenting with ADHD.
Celiac disease is more prevalent in patients with inflammatory myopathies than in the general population.
Celiac disease prevalance is significantly higher in those diagnosed with Sjogren's syndrome.
Celiac disease prevalance was found to be 3.5% in children with type 1 diabetes.
Celiac disease: hookworm infection shown to suppress mucosal inflammation
Celiac prevalence in preschool children appears to be higher than IgA/IgG antibody testing alone reveals.
Coeliac disease prevalence may be relatively common in Northern Ireland, as indicated by the 5.7% positive rate for anti-gliadin antibodies in those sampled.
Cognitive impairment in coeliac disease improves on a gluten-free diet.
Copper deficiency induced myeloneuropathy may be due to undiagnosed celiac disease.
Correlation between HLA and DED: DED resulted more frequent (100%) in atypical and silent CD forms.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: No spontaneous remission — autoimmune injury continues as long as gluten is consumed; villous atrophy persists in 30-40% at 5 years even on gluten-free diet (GFD) due to cross-contamination
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Strict gluten-free diet: histological remission (Marsh 0-1) in 60-70% at 2 years; mucosal healing complete in only ~40% at 5 years; non-responsive celiac in 7-30% (defined as persistent symptoms/histology on strict GFD)
remission.gap_size: Moderate — GFD is effective but imperfect; adherence is the primary barrier; and no pharmacotherapy exists for GFD non-responders
remission.barrier_detail: The GFD is the only treatment and no pharmacotherapy is approved, leaving 7-30% of non-responsive/refractory patients without additional options; even 'strict' adherence fails to achieve mucosal healing in a large minority due to food labelling gaps and cross-contamination; quality of life impact of GFD is substantial; refractory celiac disease Type II (RCDII) has very poor prognosis
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — FALCATA Phase 3 (larazotide, tight junction modulator) — failed primary endpoint; 2023 ATLAS Phase 2 (latiglutenase, gluten-degrading enzyme) — some histological benefit; GFD has been the only treatment since celiac disease was first described in 1950s; no pharmacotherapy approved to date
remission.outdated_treatments: Elemental diets for refractory celiac (limited evidence, highly restrictive); prolonged corticosteroid use for refractory celiac before budesonide or RCDII workup (budesonide is preferable with less systemic effect); routine HLA typing for diagnosis (DQ2/DQ8 testing is most useful for ruling out celiac, not confirming it)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Latiglutenase (KumaMax/STAN1 — gluten-degrading proteases; Phase 2 positive for reducing gluten-induced histological damage); probiotics (preliminary evidence for modulating gluten immunogenicity); larazotide acetate (tight junction regulator — repeated Phase 2/3 attempts, endpoint failures)
remission.frontier_signal: Nexvax2 (gluten peptide desensitisation vaccine — Phase 2 failed 2019 primary endpoint but proof-of-concept established); ZED-1227 (transglutaminase 2 inhibitor — Phase 2 positive 2021, reduces villous atrophy); anti-IL-15 (AMG 714 for RCDII — Phase 2); oral gluten challenge + enzyme therapy combinations
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
